steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: Left of the blank: “the observatory sites... number nearly five [BLANK] across the globe” — an independent clause. Right of the blank: “the radars track mesospheric winds and other geospace phenomena” — another independent clause. The two must be properly separated.

steps.title: Apply the rule for separating independent clauses
steps.content:
Choice B (“dozen. Located”) uses a period, properly separating the two clauses into two sentences.

The second sentence opens with the participial phrase “Located across the globe,” which correctly modifies “the radars,” and continues with the independent clause “the radars track mesospheric winds and other geospace phenomena.” Choices A, C, and D add modifying information but maintain only a comma between the two independent clauses, creating a comma splice.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
